content.full_text:
86204

Prohibition not applicable if official donates his gift

to charity.

An official may cure a potential violation of

§86204 by donating gifts of $10 or more from lobbyist to

a bona fide charity.

/n-79-210

sections.conclusion: An official may cure a potential violation of §86204 by donating gifts of $10 or more from lobbyist to a bona fide charity.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether an official can remedy a violation of Government Code Section 86204 by donating gifts received from lobbyists to a bona fide charity.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: An official can remedy a potential violation of Section 86204 by donating gifts of $10 or more received from lobbyists to a bona fide charity.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether an official can remedy a violation of Government Code Section 86204 by donating gifts received from lobbyists to a bona fide charity.

CONCLUSION: An official may cure a potential violation of §86204 by donating gifts of $10 or more from lobbyist to a bona fide charity.
embedding.first_500_words: 86204 Prohibition not applicable if official donates his gift to charity. An official may cure a potential violation of §86204 by donating gifts of $10 or more from lobbyist to a bona fide charity. /n-79-210
embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter 79-210 addresses whether officials can cure violations of Government Code Section 86204 concerning lobbyist gifts by donating such gifts to charity. The FPPC concludes that donation of gifts of $10 or more from lobbyists to bona fide charities cures the prohibition.
